messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] UNIX and Shell Scripting for Bioinformatics
messages.subject_clean: UNIX and Shell Scripting for Bioinformatics
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We are pleased to announce the upcoming Physalia online course ?UNIX and Shell Scripting for Bioinformatics?, taking place on 12?16 January 2026.
 
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/unix/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/unix/ )
 
This practical course introduces participants to the foundations of Unix and command-line environments, with a strong focus on applications in bioinformatics. Through hands-on exercises, attendees will learn how to navigate Unix systems, use essential commands, process large biological datasets, and build reproducible workflows using shell scripting. The sessions will also cover connecting to remote servers, installing software, managing environments, and working efficiently with NGS data.
 
The course assumes no prior Unix or scripting knowledge and is ideal for researchers who wish to strengthen their computational skills.
 
If you are interested in joining, please visit our course page for more details: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/unix/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/unix/ ) 
 
Kind regards,
 
Carlo
 
 
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

mobile: +49 17645230846

[ Bluesky ]( https://bsky.app/profile/physaliacourses.bsky.social ) [ Linkedin ]( https://www.linkedin.com/in/physalia-courses-a64418127/ )

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Population Genomics course
messages.body_plain:
Dear colleagues,

We are pleased to announce the upcoming Physalia online course: INTRODUCTION TO POPULATION GENOMICS
 
Dates: 16?20 March 2026
 
For further information and registration details, please visit:
[ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/population-genomics/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/population-genomics/ )
 
This five-day course offers a comprehensive introduction to key concepts and analytical methods in population genomics. The program combines lectures with hands-on practical sessions each day, enabling participants to apply techniques directly to real datasets.
 
 
Topics covered include:

? Management of short-read sequencing data: QC, trimming, mapping, SNP calling
? Population structure and introgression
? Demographic inference and model comparison
? Genome scans for association and selection (GWAS, GEA, selective sweeps)
? Landscape genomics and spatial analyses
 
The course is intended for graduate students, postdoctoral researchers, and scientists with a basic background in genetics/genomics and some familiarity with UNIX command line tools and R. 
 
For the full list of our courses and workshops, please visit: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/ )[  ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/p )
 
 
Best regards,
Carlo
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

mobile: +49 17645230846

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Genomic Data Visualisation with R
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,We are excited to announce our upcoming online course Genomic Data Visualisation with R, taking place 15?17 December 2025. Designed for participants familiar with R, this course covers essential graphical methods used in genomics research.Over three days, you will learn to create and interpret key plots such as volcano plots, genome coverage graphs, phylogenetic trees, synteny plots, and many more.Course schedule:Monday to Wednesday, 1?6 PM Berlin timeCourse website:[ https://www.physalia-courses.org/cou...omic-data-viz/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/genomic-data-viz/ )Whether your focus is expression studies, genome statistics, or comparative genomics, this course offers practical skills to enhance your data visualization toolkit.Secure your spot and join an international community of genomic researchers!Best regards,Carlo PecoraroPhysalia-courses Director
 
[ www.physalia-courses.org ]( http://www.physalia-courses.org ) 
 
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Marine Genomics course in Crete - 25 to 29 May 2026.
messages.subject_clean: Marine Genomics course in Crete - 25 to 29 May 2026.
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We are pleased to announce our upcoming in-person Physalia course on Marine Genomics, held at the Hellenic Centre for Marine Research in Heraklion, Crete, from 25 to 29 May 2026.
 
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/marine-genomics/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/marine-genomics/ ) 
 
This course offers a comprehensive introduction to the rapidly evolving field of marine  genomics, combining theoretical foundations with practical, hands-on training using real marine datasets. Over five days, participants will learn to assess genome assemblies, process sequencing data, perform population genomics analyses, and integrate genomic with environmental data in seascape genomics frameworks. The course is designed for graduate students, postdocs, and researchers with some background in genetics/genomics and evolutionary biology, as well as familiarity with R and UNIX command line.
 
Under the guidance of leading experts, you will develop skills to design and conduct marine genomics studies, interpret complex data, and apply insights to ecology, conservation, and fisheries management. This is an excellent opportunity to gain practical experience in a unique setting while connecting with peers and experts in the field.
 
Best regards,
 
Valentina
 
 
 

Valentina Sardina
Course coordinator
info at physalia-courses.org
[ http://www.physalia-courses.org/ ]( http://www.physalia-courses.org/ ) 
mobile: +49 17645230846
[ Bluesky ]( https://bsky.app/profile/physaliacourses.bsky.social ) [ Linkedin ]( https://www.linkedin.com/in/physalia-courses-a64418127/ )

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Free Instats events – happy holidays
messages.body_plain:
Hello everyone

Over the holidays Instats is pleased to be offering over 80 free research training events that you can access on-demand and livestreaming. Whether it's software introductions or modern AI methods for research, there's something for everyone in the lineup of Instats research training below.

We hope you and your colleagues enjoy?and happy holidays from everyone at Instats!

Questionnaire Design with Surette van Staden - Livestream Jan 12 <https://instats.org/seminar/questionnaire-design-principles-and-prac>

Success as an Applied Statistician with the ASA - Livestream Jan 14 <https://instats.org/seminar/success-as-an-applied-statistician>
